subject: [PATCH v4 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

reviews.author: Alison Schofield
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM, and asked if the intention is to conditionally compile it based on this configuration option.
reviews.raw_body:
This always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM.
Are you heading towards conditionally compiling, where region_pmem.o is
omitted if !CONFIG_CXL_PMEM? 

Similar question for region_dax.o

--Alison

2026-03-22.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three new files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. The goal is to make it clear that certain logic applies only to specific types of regions, reducing development conflicts and improving code clarity.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges reviewer's concern about adding more Kconfig cruft, but is unsure if it's worth the effort to save some object files and code size
reviews.raw_body:
I hadn't considered going that far, but it doesn't seem unreasonable?

It just seemed natural to break out the code this way, but I don't know
if adding more Kconfig cruft is worth it just to save some .o's and a
smaller amount of code.

The recent pmem/nvdimm Kconfig change does kind of show that we might
have some bloat for some deployments which might not care about pmem -
for example.

~Gregory

2026-03-23.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three new files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. The goal is to make it clear that certain logic applies only to specific types of regions, reducing development conflicts and improving code clarity.
